title: About Boys Who Cook
subtitle: Learn about our story, mission, and the team behind our movement.

story.heading: Our Story
story.text:
Boys Who Cook was founded with a simple but powerful idea: that teaching boys to cook can change their lives.

We started as a small community program with just a handful of participants and a shared kitchen. What began as an after-school activity quickly grew into something much bigger — a movement dedicated to empowering young men through culinary education.

Today, Boys Who Cook reaches boys across the community through classes, events, and outreach programs. We believe that when a boy learns to cook, he learns much more than recipes — he learns patience, creativity, teamwork, and self-reliance.

Our founder, Shaan, started this organization because he saw a need in his community and decided to do something about it. At just 14 years old, he believed that every boy deserves the opportunity to discover the power of cooking.

mission_vision.mission_text: To empower boys through culinary education by providing hands-on cooking experiences that build confidence, life skills, and community.
mission_vision.vision_text: A world where every young man has the skills, confidence, and support to nourish himself, his family, and his community.

values.title: Empowerment
values.description: We believe in the power of every young man to grow, lead, and make a difference through the skills they develop in the kitchen.

values.title: Community
values.description: Cooking brings people together. We build strong, supportive communities where boys can learn, share, and thrive.

values.title: Creativity
values.description: The kitchen is a place for creativity and self-expression. We encourage every participant to explore new flavors and ideas.

values.title: Service
values.description: We teach boys not just to cook for themselves, but to use their skills to serve and give back to their communities.
